fields.label: Sentry URL
fields.description: Sentry URL to be used. If left blank, https://sentry.io will be used

fields.label: Sentry Org
fields.description: Sentry Org slug. Typically this will be the last segment of the URL: https://sentry.io/organizations/{organization_slug}/ - only the slug should be entered here

fields.label: Sentry Auth Token
fields.description: Sentry authentication token. Auth tokens can be created from https://sentry.io/settings/{organization_slug}/developer-settings

fields.label: Skip TLS Verify
fields.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. Use this option for self-hosted Sentry instances with self-signed certificates.

relationships.description: Connection to a Sentry organization: the base URL, the org slug it identifies, and the Bearer auth token used against the Sentry API.

instructions.msg: The Sentry datasource authenticates against the Sentry API using a Bearer token stored in secureJsonData.authToken. Every outgoing request adds an 'Authorization: Bearer <authToken>' header (pkg/sentry/client.go:37-40). There are no other auth methods.

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"url":"https://sentry.io","orgSlug":"<org>"},"secureJsonData":{"authToken":"<token>"}}. orgSlug and authToken are strictly required — pkg/plugin/settings.go:41-50 returns 'invalid or empty organization slug' or 'empty or invalid auth token found' otherwise. jsonData.url is optional: when omitted the backend defaults it to 'https://sentry.io' (pkg/plugin/settings.go:37-40).

Auth tokens are created in the Sentry UI at https://sentry.io/settings/<organization_slug>/developer-settings — either as user auth tokens or organization auth tokens with sufficient scopes to read the org's projects, issues, events, and stats.

secureJsonData.authToken is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the token is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.authToken (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.authToken.

jsonData.orgSlug is the org slug (the last segment of https://sentry.io/organizations/{organization_slug}/), NOT the org display name or ID. It becomes part of every Sentry REST path the client builds (pkg/sentry/orgs.go, pkg/sentry/projects.go, etc.).

jsonData.url must be the base URL of the Sentry instance, without a trailing slash. The client string-concatenates request paths onto it (pkg/sentry/sentry.go:54-56 — 'sc.BaseURL + path' where path starts with '/api/0/...'), so a trailing slash produces double-slash API URLs. For self-hosted Sentry with a private CA, set jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true (pkg/plugin/plugin.go:57-63) — this disables server certificate verification entirely and should only be used with trusted self-signed certificates.
